title: -Relinquishment of Council Office Budget Funds from General Funds and Grant of Such Funds to Bay Area Robotics Education Sustainability Alliance for the 2025 Berkeley High Robotics Team’s East Bay Regional Robotics Competition.

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution approving the expenditure of an amount not to exceed $250 per
member of the City Council from the D13 Discretionary Accounts of members of the
Berkeley City Council, including $250 from Mayor Ishii and Councilmembers O’Keefe,
Taplin, and Tregub, and inviting other members of the City Council to contribute, to
support the Berkeley High Robotics Team (Berkelium 54191) in hosting the East Bay
Regional FIRST2 Robotics Competition3. Funds will be transferred to the City’s general
fund and to Bay Area Robotics Education Sustainability Alliance, a fiscal agent for the
2025 Berkeley High Robotics Team’s East Bay Regional Robotics Competition to
support this valuable community event.
The relinquishment of funds from the respective discretionary Council Office Budgets of
such members of the Berkeley City Council who wish to contribute will support this
important initiative, fostering STEM education, student leadership, and international
collaboration.
1 https://team5419.wixsite.com/team5419
2 https://www.firstinspires.org/
3 https://cafirst.org/frc/east-bay-regional/#
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7140 ● E-Mail: itregub@berkeleyca.gov
Page 2 of 4
sections.background:
The East Bay Regional FIRST Robotics Competition4, hosted annually by the Berkeley
High Robotics Team (Berkelium 54195), is a premier science, technology, engineering,
and mathematics (STEM) event that brings together high school robotics teams from
across California, the nation, and around the globe. This year, 56 teams were
competing in the exciting "Reefscape" challenge at Berkeley High School, drawing
students, mentors, parents, and supporters from all corners of the world.
The competition offers an unparalleled opportunity for students to engage in hands-on
learning, collaboration, and friendly competition in the STEM fields. The event highlights
the talents and innovations of over 80 student members of the Berkeley High Robotics
Team, which is entirely student-run with invaluable guidance from volunteer mentors.
As a student-led team without the backing of corporate sponsors or paid mentors,
Berkelium 5419 faces unique financial challenges. To ensure the event's success and
maintain its high standards, the team is working to raise $15,000-$20,000 to cover
essential costs such as event logistics, equipment, and travel expenses for both local
and international teams.
The Berkeley High Robotics Team also proudly hosts participants from Hawaii, as well
as other countries like Turkey, Japan, Australia, further elevating the event's prestige
and showcasing the City of Berkeley’s commitment to fostering global STEM education.
This event not only provides a transformative educational experience but also serves as
a point of pride for the community, drawing positive attention to Berkeley as a leader in
STEM innovation and collaboration.
sections.current_situation:
The escalating costs of hosting the East Bay Regional FIRST Robotics competition
have put significant financial pressure on the Berkeley High Robotics Team. These
costs include expenses for the venue, essential equipment, safety measures, and travel
for both local and international teams. In order to ensure the event's continued success
and to provide ongoing STEM opportunities to Berkeley students, additional funding is
crucial to cover the following needs:
 Event logistics and venue rental;
 Materials and equipment required for competition setup;
 Travel expenses for participating teams; and
4 https://team5419.wixsite.com/team5419/support-us
Page 3 of 4
 Support for both local and international teams, enhancing the event’s diversity
and scope
As the Berkeley High Robotics Team operates independently and is not categorized as
a formal school "club," it does not receive regular funding from the school district.
Therefore, financial support from the City Council is vital to the successful hosting of this
prestigious event and to ensuring that students from all backgrounds continue to benefit
from these valuable educational experiences.
FINANCIAL IMPLICATIONS
No General Fund impact. Up to $250 is available from contributing Mayor’s and
Councilmembers’ Council Office Budget discretionary accounts.

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
AUTHORIZING THE EXPENDITURE OF SURPLUS FUNDS FROM THE OFFICE
EXPENSE ACCOUNTS OF COUNCILMEMBERS FOR A GRANT TO SUPPORT THE
BERKELEY HIGH ROBOTICS TEAM’S EAST BAY REGIONAL FIRST ROBOTICS
COMPETITION
WHEREAS, Mayor Ishii and Councilmembers O’Keefe, Taplin, and Tregub have
surplus funds in their D13 discretionary office expenditure account and will contribute
$250, and invite other Councilmembers to join in contributing; and
WHEREAS, the Berkeley High Robotics Team, "Berkelium 5419," is a fully student-led
team competing in the prestigious FIRST Robotics international league, dedicated to
fostering innovation, teamwork, and STEM education among high school students; and
WHEREAS, from April 3-6, 2025, Berkelium 5419, along with their newly established
Freshman team, "Bearium 5499," hosted the East Bay Regional FIRST Robotics
Competition at Berkeley High School, attracting 56 teams from across California and
Hawaii, and the world, including international teams from Turkey, Japan, Australia, to
compete in the "Reefscape" challenge; and
WHEREAS, this event brings recognition and prestige to Berkeley High School and the
City of Berkeley, highlighting student achievements and fostering collaboration among
local and international teams; and
WHEREAS, the successful execution of this large-scale competition requires additional
funding to cover logistics, materials, equipment, and operational expenses, with an
estimated funding need of $15,000 to $20,000; and
WHEREAS, as a student-run team without school district funding, the Berkeley High
Robotics Team relies on community donations, sponsorships, and fundraising to cover
costs associated with participation and event hosting; and
WHEREAS, the provision of such funding fulfills the municipal public purpose of
supporting science, technology, e
